summary: Mixed matrix recording – first set audience‑only and noisy, second set superb soundboard; strong mid‑80s show with standout Let It Grow, Morning Dew and Eyes of the World
review: The recording is a split source: the opening set is an audience tape plagued by high‑end imbalance, clicks and drop‑outs, while the second set is a clean sound‑board capture that many fans describe as “super sounding.” Musically the night shines in the middle‑80s style – the Let It Grow stretch, a soaring Morning Dew opener for the second set, and an especially pretty Eyes of the World are repeatedly praised, though some listeners note a sloppy Deal opener and a puzzling Baby Blue encore.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is generally good with several highlighted songs, but the first‑set recording issues drag the overall experience down; enough detailed reviews give a confident combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Set 1: audience (AUD) – overly bright cymbals, drop‑outs, clicks, levels too high; difficult to listen. Set 2: soundboard (SBD) – clear, balanced, “super sounding” with good mids and low noise.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual opening with Deal (audience‑only) followed by a more conventional second set that kicks off with Morning Dew; the flow is quirky but the second half delivers strong transitions and highlights.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocal performance gets special praise – “more to offer vocally than Weir.”
